meta.title: Firing program library by intent
meta.note: Programs are controller-style templates. They are starting points that must be validated with witness cones, kiln behavior, and actual load density.

programs.name: Standard candled bisque
bestFor: general stoneware and porcelain bisque loads
moderate wall thickness and mixed ware
outcomeGoals: finish water-smoking safely
prepare body for even glaze uptake
riskSignals: steam-crack evidence in thick work
body still gassing heavily in glaze firing
adjustments: increase early holds for thicker or wetter loads
reduce ramp speed in the 572F to 1112F band if carbon burnout issues persist

programs.name: Low-fire bright clear
outcomeGoals: maintain high transparency and brightness
reduce micro-bubble haze at low fire
riskSignals: cloudy clear from under-melt or excessive thickness
pinholes from incomplete burnout before seal
adjustments: if haze persists, extend final hold modestly
if color mutes, reduce over-thickness before modifying program

programs.name: Low-fire fit and bubble-heal
bestFor: fit-focused low-fire tests where bubble healing is needed
outcomeGoals: improve low-fire surface healing without over-driving fluidity
support clearer fit comparisons between low-fire branches
riskSignals: body overfire on thin low-fire ware
unwanted softening of decoration at higher low-fire cones
adjustments: if body is stressed, drop peak slightly and maintain hold profile
if bubbles remain, verify bisque burnout before increasing peak

programs.name: Cone-6 clear basic
bestFor: stable cone-6 clear and host tests
outcomeGoals: provide a neutral baseline for clear comparisons
avoid over-complicated cooling influence in first rounds
riskSignals: micro-bubbles remain in tight clear systems
color host tests conflate schedule and chemistry if this baseline is skipped
adjustments: move to drop-and-hold profile if bubbles persist
use witness-cone placement near test ware, not only shelf corners

programs.name: Cone-6 clear drop-and-hold
bestFor: liner and warm-color host comparison panels
outcomeGoals: heal micro-bubbles after maturity
improve transparency in cone-6 clear families
riskSignals: excessive running if glaze is already very fluid
false confidence if witness cones are not checked
adjustments: reduce top hold if fluid glazes begin to run
if bubbles remain, slow early climb and review bisque quality

programs.name: Cone-6 matte slow cool
bestFor: rutile and iron variation in controlled matte systems
outcomeGoals: open matte texture and satin balance
improve visual depth in matte and variegated surfaces
riskSignals: matte turns chalky if chemistry is already too stiff
unexpected cutlery marking increase in under-tuned matte systems
adjustments: if surface over-mattes, shorten controlled-cool segment
if glaze is too glossy, keep chemistry fixed and increase slow-cool influence first

programs.name: Cone-6 floating blue break
outcomeGoals: develop cream breaking without fully flattening blue pools
map thickness sensitivity under repeatable schedule conditions
riskSignals: run risk rises sharply above medium-heavy application
break effect collapses under very thin coatings
adjustments: if excessive run appears, reduce hold at drop stage before changing recipe
if break is weak, verify thickness and texture before increasing cobalt or rutile

programs.name: Cone-6 iron-red development
bestFor: schedule-first tuning for iron reds and rust-red transitions
outcomeGoals: bias toward stronger iron-red development
separate schedule effects from recipe effects in iron systems
riskSignals: if host is wrong, longer hold just deepens brown
surface can over-darken in thick zones
adjustments: if red is weak, compare host branch before raising iron
if glaze blackens, reduce thickness and confirm cooling profile

programs.name: Cone-6 reactive general
bestFor: reactive amber, rutile, and fluid-midfire comparison sets
outcomeGoals: provide a repeatable schedule for reactive comparison
avoid overloading cooling complexity in early variant rounds
riskSignals: reactive branches can run unpredictably on vertical ware
under-tested reactive hosts can be mistaken for functional glazes
adjustments: if run risk dominates, drop hold time before rewriting chemistry
if surface is sealed with bubbles, shift to drop-and-hold clear program

programs.name: Cone-6 clean celadon
bestFor: light celadon and low-iron transparent branches
outcomeGoals: keep transparency while supporting gentle celadon pool depth
reduce avoidable haze in low-iron hosts
riskSignals: dark body contribution can mask schedule intent
too-thick application destroys clean celadon read
adjustments: if celadon muddies, verify body and thickness before changing iron
if too pale, increase coat consistency before colorant

programs.name: Cone-10 reduction functional baseline
bestFor: cone-10 reduction clear, matte, and iron-family comparison
outcomeGoals: deliver repeatable high-fire reduction maturity
provide baseline for cone-10 surface family comparisons
riskSignals: reduction control drift can invalidate glaze comparisons
overfired loads may hide useful recipe distinctions
adjustments: record reduction window notes with every test run
if maturity is uncertain, rely on witness-cone position data, not controller screen only

programs.name: Overglaze refire low-temperature baseline
bestFor: overglaze color, decal, and luster exploration
outcomeGoals: fuse overglaze layer without over-softening underlying glaze
standardize first-pass refire behavior
riskSignals: surface contamination can mimic overfire defects
overlong holds can flatten luster or decoration detail
adjustments: if gloss shifts unexpectedly, reduce top hold before lowering peak
keep decoration thickness constant while tuning refire
